title: Brass
description: Arrival in the steampunk world. Suburban gear transforms into brass and clockwork. Cogsworth Junction, the clock tower chiming thirteen.

lines: The gears were already turning when they arrived. Not starting — already turning. As though the world had been running before they got there and would continue after they left.
Cogsworth Junction smelled like oil and copper. The sky was the color of old pennies. Every surface was brass or iron.
A clock tower stood at the center of everything. It was too tall. Not architecturally — philosophically.

steps.text: It chimed as they arrived. Thirteen times. There are no thirteen o'clocks. They were being announced.

lines.text: The Security Guard felt the baton grow heavy, then reshape itself. His uniform stiffened into something darker, with buckles and clockwork clasps. He was a Steamknight now.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: It's not. It's the same as the last time. Just different clothes.
The School Nurse became the Steampriest. The Science Nerd became the Alchemist. The Skater Kid became the Saboteur. The Band Kid became the Busker.
They remembered everything. Harmonia, Mordaine, Maple Heights, the Coordinator. And remembering was a variable the system had not planned for.
